back_translation: Jesus also spoke again to his disciples, saying, "A certain rich man had a household steward, and that steward was reported to have been squandering his master's possessions.
The rich man therefore summoned that steward and said to him, 'What is this that I am hearing about you? Submit the account of your management, for you will no longer be able to continue as steward.'

The steward then said within himself, 'What shall I do? For my master is taking away my position as steward from me. I do not have strength to dig the earth; to become a beggar would be shameful to me.
I know what I will do, so that when I am dismissed from my position as steward, there will be people who welcome me into their own houses.'
So the steward summoned his master's debtors one by one, and said to the first, 'How much do you owe my master?'
The debtor answered, 'One hundred baths of olive oil.' The steward then said to him, 'Take back your bill-document; sit down and quickly rewrite it as fifty.'

Then he spoke to another, saying, 'And you, how much do you owe?' That one answered, 'One hundred cors of wheat.' The steward then said to him, 'Take back your bill-document and rewrite it as eighty.'
The master commended that unrighteous steward for having acted shrewdly, because the sons of this age are more shrewd in matters concerning their own kind than the sons of light.
I tell you, use unrighteous wealth to make friends for yourselves, so that when it is gone, there will be people who welcome you into eternal dwellings.
Whoever is faithful in a small thing will also be faithful in a great thing, and whoever is not faithful in a small thing will also not be faithful in a great thing.
Therefore, if you have not been faithful in the matter of unrighteous wealth, who will then trustingly entrust true riches to you?
And if you have not been faithful with the wealth that belongs to another, who will grant to you wealth that is your own?
No household-servant is able to serve two masters. For he will hate one master and love the other, or he will hold firm to one master and despise the other. You cannot serve both God and wealth."
The Pharisees, who were lovers of money, heard all these things and sneered at him.
He therefore said to them, "You are those who consider yourselves righteous before people, but God knows your hearts. For what is exalted among people is an abomination in the sight of God.

The law and the words of the prophets existed until the time of John. From that time, the good news concerning the kingdom of God has been preached, and everyone is striving with full effort to enter into that kingdom.
But it is easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for one small stroke of the law to fall away.
Whoever divorces his wife and marries another woman commits adultery, and whoever marries a woman who has been divorced by her husband likewise commits adultery.
There was also a certain rich man, who clothed himself with purple cloth and fine-quality linen, and lived luxuriously every day.
There was also a certain beggar named Lazarus, who was laid at the gate of the rich man's house, having sores all over his body,
and he longed to eat the scraps of food that fell from the rich man's table. But worse still, the dogs came and licked his sores.
It came to pass that the beggar died, and the angels carried him to the bosom of Abraham. The rich man also died and was buried.
That rich man was in the realm of the dead and was suffering in torment. When he lifted his face up, he saw Abraham far away and Lazarus in the bosom of Abraham.

The rich man therefore cried out, saying, 'Honorable ancestor-father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus to dip the tip of his finger in water, to cool my tongue; for I am in torment in this flame.'
But Abraham answered, 'Child, remember that during your lifetime you received your good things, and Lazarus received only bad things. But now he receives comfort here, while you suffer in torment.
Moreover, between us and you there is a great chasm firmly established, so that those who wish to cross from here over to you cannot, and so that no one can cross from there over to us at all.'
The rich man therefore said, 'Honorable ancestor-father, if so, I beg you earnestly: please send Lazarus to the house of my father,
for I have five brothers. Let Lazarus warn them, so that they will not have to come to this place of torment also.'
Abraham answered, 'They already have Moses and the prophets; let them listen.'
He answered, 'Not so, honorable ancestor-father Abraham; but if someone from among the dead goes to them, they will repent.'
But Abraham answered him, 'If they do not listen to Moses and the prophets, even if someone rises up from among the dead, they will not believe.'"
